question:
US x Venezuela military engagement by November 2?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military engagement between the military forces of the United States of America and Venezuela between August 21, and November 2, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military engagement" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Venezuelan military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution; however, minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not. 

Any U.S. military kinetic strike that impacts Venezuelan land territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

Missiles or drone strikes which are intercepted will not qualify regardless of whether they land on adversarial territory or cause damage. Similarly, surface-to-air missile strikes impacting adversarial territory will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution (although surface to air missiles fired at an adversarial military aircraft would qualify).

Note: the U.S. Coast Guard is a branch of the U.S. Armed Forces; Venezuela’s Milicia Nacional Bolivariana (militia) is a “special component” of the Venezuelan National Bolivarian Armed Forces (FANB); and Venezuela’s Coast Guard (Comando de Guardacostas) is a component of the Venezuelan Navy (Armada Bolivariana) and thus part of the FANB.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.
label: NO
rationale: The provided evidence indicates heightened U.S.-Venezuela tensions and significant U.S. military presence in the Caribbean, but it does not report any confirmed direct kinetic military engagement between U.S. and Venezuelan forces during the relevant period. The closest item describes a Venezuelan fighter shadowing a U.S. Navy aircraft in 2019, which is not a qualifying engagement under the market definition. A congressional resolution reference mentions U.S. actions 'within or against Venezuela' as hostilities or imminent hostilities, but that is not evidence of an actual engagement. Absent credible reporting of strikes, gunfire, ramming, or other direct force, the most likely outcome is No.

question:
Will Israel strike Gaza on November 9?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Gaza soil on the listed date Israel Standard Time (IST). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact Gaza ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of the Gaza Strip counts, including buffer zones.

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Gaza territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary solution resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting within 48 hours of this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.
label: YES
rationale: The evidence provided does not include a dated, credible report or official statement confirming that Israel initiated an air/drone/missile strike on Gaza specifically on November 9. Most documents are general background on past Gaza operations or ceasefire-related context, not date-specific confirmation for the market date. With no direct evidence of a qualifying strike on that day, uncertainty remains high but the best-supported choice is No under the market rules.

question:
Will Russia capture Myrnohrad by November 15?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the intersection between Vatutina Vulytsya and Puhachova Vulytsya located in Myrnohrad by November 15, 2025, at 11:59 PM ET.

The intersection station will be considered captured if any part of the intersection is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by November 15, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the intersection, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Intersection Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/myrnohrad+intersection+zoom.png

Intersection Location in Myrnohrad: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/myrnohrad+intersection.jpeg

Myrnohrad Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/myrnohrad.jpeg

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/qCLFQNiDKN2ABe5Q9

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.
label: YES
rationale: ISW reporting through Nov. 14 says Russian forces had recently advanced in southern Myrnohrad and were continuing to advance within the Pokrovsk–Myrnohrad pocket. Earlier Nov. 8 reporting already indicated marginal Russian advances into northern Myrnohrad, suggesting Russia is at or very near the relevant area by the cutoff. However, the evidence does not explicitly confirm that the specific intersection is shaded red/captured on the ISW map by Nov. 15, so there remains meaningful uncertainty.

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual meets with Donald Trump between November 1 and November 30, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both the listed individual and Trump are present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.
label: YES
rationale: Credible reporting indicates Trump and Ahmed al-Sharaa already met on May 14, 2025, and multiple November 2025 reports state Trump is scheduled to host al-Sharaa at the White House on November 10, which falls within the market window. That makes a meeting in November highly likely and effectively reported as planned by multiple outlets.

question:
US x Venezuela military engagement by October 27?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military engagement between the military forces of the United States of America and Venezuela by  October 27, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military engagement" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Venezuelan military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution; however, minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not. 

Any U.S. military kinetic strike that impacts Venezuelan land territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

Missiles or drone strikes which are intercepted will not qualify regardless of whether they land on adversarial territory or cause damage. Similarly, surface-to-air missile strikes impacting adversarial territory will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution (although surface to air missiles fired at an adversarial military aircraft would qualify).

Note: the U.S. Coast Guard is a branch of the U.S. Armed Forces; Venezuela’s Milicia Nacional Bolivariana (militia) is a “special component” of the Venezuelan National Bolivarian Armed Forces (FANB); and Venezuela’s Coast Guard (Comando de Guardacostas) is a component of the Venezuelan Navy (Armada Bolivariana) and thus part of the FANB.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.
label: NO
rationale: The provided evidence shows heightened U.S.-Venezuela regional tension and U.S. military activity in the Caribbean/Guyana context, but it does not indicate any direct kinetic military engagement between U.S. and Venezuelan armed forces. Several sources discuss exercises, posture statements, surveillance flights, and Venezuela raising alert levels, which are not sufficient under the market definition. No cited evidence reports missile strikes, gunfire, ship ramming, or other direct use of force between the two militaries by the resolution date.

question:
Will Israel strike Lebanon on November 1?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Lebanese soil on the listed date Israel Standard Time (IST). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact Lebanon ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of Lebanon counts, including buffer zones.

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Lebanon territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary solution resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting within 48 hours of this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.
label: YES
rationale: The evidence shows an ongoing pattern of Israeli air/drone strikes in Lebanon, including a rare strike on Beirut and an intensified air campaign throughout November 2024. Given this context, an Israeli strike on Lebanese soil on November 1 appears more likely than not. However, the provided evidence does not confirm a strike specifically on that date, so uncertainty remains.

question:
Will 'The Fantastic Four: First Steps' have the best domestic opening weekend in 2025?

Description: This is a market on how whether 'The Fantastic Four: First Steps' (2025) will gross more domestically on its opening weekend than any other movie in 2025. The “Weekend Box Office Performance” table on https://www.the-numbers.com/movie/Fantastic-Four-The-First-Steps-(2025)#tab=box-office will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day opening weekend (July 25 - 27) are final (i.e. not studio estimates).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if 'The Fantastic Four: First Steps' (2025) grosses more on its 3-day domestic opening weekend than any other movie in 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Please note, this market will resolve according to the The Numbers figures provided under Weekend Box Office Performance for the 3-day weekend, regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

Resolution will be based specifically on the figures provided for this movie's 3-day opening weekend (Friday, Saturday, Sunday), regardless of whether the movie has a 4 or 5-day opening weekend.

If another movie's opening weekend box office performance surpasses that of 'The Fantastic Four: First Steps' (2025) after numbers for both are finalized, this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If this movie's opening weekend box office performance ties with any other's, the movie whose title comes first in alphabetical order will win.

If there is no final data available by January 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.
label: YES
rationale: Available pre-release tracking from multiple box office sources consistently places The Fantastic Four: First Steps in the $100M-$136M domestic opening range, with several forecasts above Superman and other 2025 releases cited in the evidence. That makes it a strong candidate to lead the year’s domestic opening weekends. However, this is still a forecast and could be challenged by an unexpectedly larger opener later in 2025, so confidence is good but not overwhelming.

question:
Will "Wicked: For Good" Opening Weekend Box Office be between 150m and 163m?

Description: This market will resolve according to how much "Wicked: For Good" Opening Weekend Box Office will gross domestically on its opening weekend. The "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day opening weekend (November 21 - 23) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the The Numbers figures provided under Weekend Box Office Performance for the 3-day weekend (which typically includes Thursday's previews), regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is ambiguity as to whether the resolution source's figures are final, this market will remain open until both https://www.boxofficemojo.com/ and https://www.the-numbers.com/ have confirmed their finalized figures.

If there is no final data available by December 1, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.
label: YES
rationale: The strongest cited forecasts center around the $150M-$164M range: Box Office Theory first lists a $145M forecast, then later updates to $163.7M for the 3-day weekend, which falls inside the target bracket. Boxoffice Pro’s long-range headline also indicates the film is on pace to surpass the first Wicked’s debut weekend forecast, and the presales/marketing signals cited by Deadline and NBCUniversal suggest strong opening demand. While some earlier mainstream estimates were lower ($112M-$115M), the more recent and more specific forecast evidence points to a result within the asked range, though uncertainty remains because forecasts can overshoot actuals.

question:
Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by November 30?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by November 30, 2025, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market. 

A peace deal or political framework will qualify if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, effective on a specific date. Frameworks or agreements that outline terms for a future peace but do not include an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not count.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.
label: NO
rationale: The evidence suggests ongoing conflict and repeated proposals for ceasefires, but no official, mutually agreed Russia-Ukraine ceasefire has been documented. Several sources indicate Russia has rejected or undermined ceasefire efforts, and the cited agreements in the materials are either unilateral temporary truces, humanitarian arrangements, or unrelated maritime deals rather than a publicly announced bilateral halt in military engagement. Based on the provided evidence, a ceasefire by Nov. 30, 2025 appears unlikely.

question:
US x Venezuela military engagement by November 30?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military engagement between the military forces of the United States of America and Venezuela between August 21, and November 30, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military engagement" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Venezuelan military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution; however, minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not. 

Any U.S. military kinetic strike that impacts Venezuelan land territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

Missiles or drone strikes which are intercepted will not qualify regardless of whether they land on adversarial territory or cause damage. Similarly, surface-to-air missile strikes impacting adversarial territory will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution (although surface to air missiles fired at an adversarial military aircraft would qualify).

Note: the U.S. Coast Guard is a branch of the U.S. Armed Forces; Venezuela’s Milicia Nacional Bolivariana (militia) is a “special component” of the Venezuelan National Bolivarian Armed Forces (FANB); and Venezuela’s Coast Guard (Comando de Guardacostas) is a component of the Venezuelan Navy (Armada Bolivariana) and thus part of the FANB.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.
label: NO
rationale: The evidence shows a substantial U.S. military buildup and heightened tension around Venezuela, plus U.S. strikes on alleged drug boats in international waters. However, the provided sources do not report a direct kinetic military engagement between U.S. and Venezuelan military forces as defined in the market. No credible report here describes gunfire, missile/artillery exchange, ship ramming with significant damage, or a U.S. kinetic strike impacting Venezuelan territory through Nov. 30, 2025. Based on the evidence provided, a 'No' resolution is more likely.

question:
Will Israel strike Lebanon on November 4?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Lebanese soil on the listed date Israel Standard Time (IST). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact Lebanon ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of Lebanon counts, including buffer zones.

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Lebanon territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary solution resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting within 48 hours of this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.
label: YES
rationale: The evidence shows a pattern of repeated Israeli airstrikes on Lebanon, including credible reporting and UN references to ongoing violations and strikes on Lebanese territory. While the provided sources do not specifically confirm a strike on November 4, the market asks for whether Israel will strike Lebanon on that date, and the recent pattern of near-daily/ongoing strikes makes a strike more likely than not. Uncertainty remains because the date-specific event is not directly evidenced here.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.
label: NO
rationale: The evidence shows a significant U.S. military buildup and heightened tension with Venezuela in November 2025, but it does not provide credible reporting of an actual kinetic military engagement between U.S. and Venezuelan forces during the resolution window. The cited reports describe deployments, statements, and prior aerial shadowing incidents, which are not sufficient for a Yes under the market rules. Absent direct evidence of gunfire, missile strikes, impacts on territory, or similar force between the forces, the most likely outcome is No.

question:
Will 1 Fed rate cut happen in 2025?

Description: The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the there is exactly 1 cut of 25 basis points by the Fed's December meeting (including any cuts made during Dec meeting). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

This market may resolve to "Yes" immediately after the statement from the Fed's December 2025 meeting has been released if there has been exactly 1 rate cut by then.

For example, if the FED cuts rates by 50 bps after a meeting, it would be considered 2 cuts (of 25 bps each). If there are 2 or more rate cuts at any point before this market's resolution date, this market will resolve to "No".

Note that cuts between 1-24 bps (inclusive) will also be considered 1 rate cut.

The resolution source for this market will be FOMC statements after meetings scheduled in 2025 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm. The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.
label: YES
rationale: The evidence shows the Fed already cut rates by 25 bps in September 2025, which constitutes exactly one cut so far. The September FOMC announcement from J.P. Morgan Asset Management states the FOMC voted to cut the target range by 25 bps to 4.00%–4.25%. The Fed's September projections also indicate a lower year-end rate path than in June, supporting the likelihood of at least one cut in 2025. Since the market resolves YES if there is exactly one 25 bps cut by the December 2025 meeting and any additional cut would make it NO, the key uncertainty is whether the Fed makes another cut later in 2025. Based on the provided evidence, a single cut already occurred and there is no direct evidence here of a second cut, so YES is favored but not certain.

question:
Will Russia recapture territory in Kucheriv Yar by November 30?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Kucheriv Yar by November 30, 2025, at 11:59 PM ET.

Territory will be considered captured if any part of Kucheriv Yar is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

Once Russia captures territory of Kucheriv Yar, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the territory of Kucheriv Yar as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap is rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.
label: YES
rationale: The evidence indicates Russia had already captured part of Kucheriv Yar by August 11, 2025, and the market only needs any part of the settlement to be shaded red on the ISW map by the resolution date. Forbes reports Russian troops advanced into Kucheriv Yar and established temporary footholds, and later reporting indicates Ukraine recaptured Kucheriv Yar, implying prior Russian capture occurred. While the exact current ISW shading by November 30 is not provided, the existence of prior Russian control makes a Yes outcome more likely than not unless the ISW map later failed to show it; the evidence still supports a substantial probability of continued/recaptured Russian control at some point before resolution.

question:
US x Venezuela military engagement by November 1?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military engagement between the military forces of the United States of America and Venezuela between August 21, and November 1, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military engagement" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Venezuelan military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution; however, minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not. 

Any U.S. military kinetic strike that impacts Venezuelan land territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

Missiles or drone strikes which are intercepted will not qualify regardless of whether they land on adversarial territory or cause damage. Similarly, surface-to-air missile strikes impacting adversarial territory will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution (although surface to air missiles fired at an adversarial military aircraft would qualify).

Note: the U.S. Coast Guard is a branch of the U.S. Armed Forces; Venezuela’s Milicia Nacional Bolivariana (militia) is a “special component” of the Venezuelan National Bolivarian Armed Forces (FANB); and Venezuela’s Coast Guard (Comando de Guardacostas) is a component of the Venezuelan Navy (Armada Bolivariana) and thus part of the FANB.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.
label: NO
rationale: The provided evidence shows U.S. and Venezuelan military presence, sanctions, and strategic tension, but no reported kinetic military engagement between U.S. and Venezuelan forces during the market window. The most relevant sources describe posture, exercises, and general risks of escalation, not an actual exchange of fire, strike, ramming, or other direct force. With no credible reporting of an incident meeting the resolution criteria, the probability of a 'Yes' is low.

question:
Will 'Wicked: For Good' have the best domestic opening weekend in 2025?

Description: This is a market on how whether 'Wicked: For Good' (2025) will gross more domestically on its opening weekend than any other movie in 2025. The “Weekend Box Office Performance” table on https://www.the-numbers.com/movie/Wicked-For-Good-(2025)#tab=box-office will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day opening weekend (November 21 - 23) are final (i.e. not studio estimates).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if 'Wicked: For Good' (2025) grosses more on its 3-day domestic opening weekend than any other movie in 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Please note, this market will resolve according to the The Numbers figures provided under Weekend Box Office Performance for the 3-day weekend, regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

Resolution will be based specifically on the figures provided for this movie's 3-day opening weekend (Friday, Saturday, Sunday), regardless of whether the movie has a 4 or 5-day opening weekend.

If another movie's opening weekend box office performance surpasses that of 'Wicked: For Good' (2025) after numbers for both are finalized, this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If this movie's opening weekend box office performance ties with any other's, the movie whose title comes first in alphabetical order will win.

If there is no final data available by January 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.
label: YES
rationale: The provided evidence strongly indicates Wicked: For Good is expected to post the biggest domestic opening weekend of 2025. Multiple box office forecast sources place it at or above the current benchmark: Boxoffice Pro says it is poised to post the biggest opening weekend of the year and forecasts $145M–$175M, while Box Office Theory projects $163.7M. Historical competition cited in the evidence notes that the top 2025 opener to beat is A Minecraft Movie at about $162M, and these forecasts are around or above that level. No evidence suggests another 2025 release is likely to exceed it after final figures are in.

question:
Israel strikes Iran by November 30?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate by November 30, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an Israeli missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.
label: YES
rationale: Credible reporting shows Israel already carried out direct airstrikes on Iranian soil in October 2024, including strikes on Iranian missile/drone sites and air defenses. The remaining question is whether this will happen again by Nov. 30, 2025. The evidence provided includes multiple 2025 sources discussing possible or expected Israeli strikes on Iran, suggesting continued elevated risk of recurrence. Given that the market only needs one qualifying Israeli drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian territory or an official Iranian diplomatic site before the deadline, the prior precedent plus ongoing escalation risk makes a Yes outcome more likely than not, though still uncertain.

question:
US x Venezuela military engagement by November 5?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military engagement between the military forces of the United States of America and Venezuela between August 21, and November 5, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military engagement" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Venezuelan military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution; however, minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not. 

Any U.S. military kinetic strike that impacts Venezuelan land territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

Missiles or drone strikes which are intercepted will not qualify regardless of whether they land on adversarial territory or cause damage. Similarly, surface-to-air missile strikes impacting adversarial territory will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution (although surface to air missiles fired at an adversarial military aircraft would qualify).

Note: the U.S. Coast Guard is a branch of the U.S. Armed Forces; Venezuela’s Milicia Nacional Bolivariana (militia) is a “special component” of the Venezuelan National Bolivarian Armed Forces (FANB); and Venezuela’s Coast Guard (Comando de Guardacostas) is a component of the Venezuelan Navy (Armada Bolivariana) and thus part of the FANB.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.
label: NO
rationale: The evidence shows a heightened U.S. military posture near Venezuela and reporting of U.S. strikes on alleged drug-trafficking boats, but none of the provided sources credibly document a direct military engagement between U.S. and Venezuelan military forces as defined here. The strongest source evidence points to deployments, sanctions, and strategic signaling rather than confirmed kinetic exchange with Venezuelan forces or strikes on Venezuelan territory. Given the definition requires direct force between the forces themselves or U.S. kinetic strikes impacting Venezuelan land, the probability of a qualifying incident is low based on the evidence provided.

Description: This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final High price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT High prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance ETH/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.
label: NO
rationale: The evidence suggests ETH has been capable of reaching and exceeding $4,000 historically, including a prior peak above that level and bullish catalysts like ETF adoption and network upgrades. However, the most recent price history provided shows monthly ETH/USD levels around the low-to-mid $3,000s in late 2024 and early 2025, and one cited long-term forecast specifically places $4,000 only by end-2026 rather than in the target month. Since the market requires a Binance 1-minute high at or above $4,000 during November, the probability is below even but still material.

question:
Will the Fed cut-pause-pause in 2025?

Description: The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve according to the decisions made by the next three Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings in 2025 (September 16-17, October 28-29, and December 9-10).

A qualifying cut occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is lower compared to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

A qualifying hike occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is higher compared to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

A qualifying pause occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is equal to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

If the Fed publishes a different combination than any listed, this market will resolve to "Other".

Emergency rate cuts outside the regularly scheduled meetings will not be considered. 

Any rate hike will be encompassed by "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meetings: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm

If no statement is released for the December meeting by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".
label: YES
rationale: The evidence shows the Fed cut rates at the September 17, 2025 FOMC meeting, lowering the target range to 4.00%-4.25%. The remaining two scheduled 2025 meetings (October and December) are therefore most plausibly pauses if no further action is taken. Since the market resolves YES only for cut-pause-pause, and the provided evidence points to September cut plus expectations/framework indicating no clear evidence of additional hikes or cuts before year-end, YES is the most likely outcome. There is still uncertainty because October and December meeting outcomes are not directly documented in the evidence, but the balance of evidence favors a cut followed by two pauses.

question:

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Huliaipole by November 30, 2025, at 11:59 PM ET.

Territory will be considered captured if any part of Huliaipole shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

Once Russia captures any of the specified territory, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

label: YES
rationale: ISW reporting through Nov. 25, 2025 shows Russian forces steadily advancing in the Hulyaipole direction, with Ukrainian statements acknowledging Russian advances and some withdrawals in nearby settlements. question:
Israel x Hamas Ceasefire Phase II by November 30?

Description: On October 8, an agreement on an initial ceasefire (Phase 1) was announced, constituting a halt in fighting, a partial Israeli pullback to a “yellow line,” prisoner swaps, expanded aid, and the release of hostages. A second phase of the deal, yet to be negotiated, is expected to address further Israeli withdrawal, Hamas’s disarmament, and the establishment of new security and governance arrangements in Gaza.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if an official agreement for the second phase of the Israel–Hamas ceasefire, defined as a publicly announced mutual agreement, is reached between Israel and Hamas by November 30, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

For purposes of this market, a “second phase” ceasefire agreement refers to a deal publicly acknowledged by both Israel and Hamas (directly or via recognized mediators such as the U.S., Egypt, or Qatar) that goes beyond the Phase 1 initial truce and includes commitments towards a comprehensive or “full” ceasefire.  Any agreement that is publicly described by the parties, or by mediators on their behalf, as the completion of “Phase 2” of the ceasefire will qualify, regardless of whether it includes further Israeli withdrawals beyond the “yellow line,” or agreements by Hamas to disarm.

An extension or continuation of the Phase 1 arrangement alone will not qualify.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Israel and Hamas, however a wide consensus of credible media reporting will also qualify.
label: NO
rationale: The evidence supports that only Phase 1 was publicly agreed in October 2025, while the second phase remained to be negotiated and was described as uncertain/problematic. Credible sources (UN, Reuters/AP-linked reporting, CSIS, Congress) indicate an initial ceasefire and hostage-release deal, but no official public mutual agreement on Phase 2 is shown. Since the market requires a publicly announced agreement specifically for the second phase by Nov. 30, 2025, the current evidence points to No unless new post-Oct. 2025 reporting emerges.

question:
US x Venezuela military engagement by November 24?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military engagement between the military forces of the United States of America and Venezuela between November 14, and November 24, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military engagement" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Venezuelan military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution; however, minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not. 

Any U.S. military kinetic strike that impacts Venezuelan land territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

Missiles or drone strikes which are intercepted will not qualify regardless of whether they land on adversarial territory or cause damage. Similarly, surface-to-air missile strikes impacting adversarial territory will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution (although surface to air missiles fired at an adversarial military aircraft would qualify).

Note: the U.S. Coast Guard is a branch of the U.S. Armed Forces; Venezuela’s Milicia Nacional Bolivariana (militia) is a “special component” of the Venezuelan National Bolivarian Armed Forces (FANB); and Venezuela’s Coast Guard (Comando de Guardacostas) is a component of the Venezuelan Navy (Armada Bolivariana) and thus part of the FANB.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.
label: NO
rationale: The provided evidence shows elevated U.S.-Venezuela tensions and U.S. naval presence in the Caribbean, but it does not report any actual kinetic military engagement between U.S. and Venezuelan forces during the relevant November 14-24, 2025 window. The strongest sources here describe posturing, exercises, and prior incidents involving Venezuela and Guyana, plus discussion of hypothetical or legal issues around U.S. action in Venezuela, not a confirmed exchange of fire, strike, or other direct force between U.S. and Venezuelan military forces. Based on the evidence provided, a qualifying engagement appears unlikely and unreported.

question:
Will NVIDIA (NVDA) beat quarterly earnings?

Description: As of market creation, NVIDIA is estimated to release earnings on November 19, 2025. The Street consensus estimate for NVIDIA’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $1.25 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if NVIDIA reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $1.25 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If NVIDIA releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

Resolution source: https://seekingalpha.com/
label: YES
rationale: The evidence points to NVIDIA likely beating the Street EPS bar of $1.25. Multiple sources indicate strong ongoing AI demand and capacity expansion at TSMC, which supports NVIDIA’s data-center demand outlook. Most directly, one source cites Seeking Alpha consensus EPS for the quarter at exactly $1.25, implying the bar is modest and that any slight upside would trigger a Yes. NVIDIA also has a strong historical pattern of beating earnings expectations, with a reported 21 beats in its last 23 quarters. Given this setup and the generally positive supply-chain and demand signals, a modest beat is more likely than not, though not certain.

question:
Will Google dip to $235 in November?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during November 2025 (ET), any 1-minute candle for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during regular trading hours (ET) will be considered.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) "Low" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/GOOGL/, with the chart settings on "1m" for candle intervals.

Resolution source: https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/GOOGL/
label: NO
rationale: GOOGL was already trading far above $235 in late October/November 2025, with evidence citing near-record prices above $268 and even an all-time high above $326 intraday on Nov. 25. Alphabet’s Q3 2025 results also showed strong revenue growth, which reduces the likelihood of a drop to $235 during regular trading hours in November. The target would require a material decline from prevailing levels, so the chance is low.

question:
Will Tesla reach $578 in November?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during November 2025 (ET), any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during regular trading hours (ET) will be considered.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "High" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TSLA/, with the chart settings on "1m" for candle intervals.

Resolution source: https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TSLA/
label: NO
rationale: The evidence suggests Tesla’s stock was trading well below $578 in late 2025, with analyst targets cited around $300 and commentary discussing levels in the $400s, not near $578. While Tesla can be volatile, there is no evidence provided of a split-adjusted move anywhere close to $578 during November 2025, so the probability of a 1-minute regular-hours high reaching at least that level is low.

question:
Tesla launches unsupervised full self driving (FSD) by October 31?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if Tesla publicly releases an unsupervised Full Self-Driving capability that satisfies all of the following criteria by October 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

1. Tesla (or Elon Musk) must label the feature “Full Self-Driving” or use an equivalent name indicating no requirement for human intervention.

2. The system must operate on public roads without a human driver present or required to oversee its performance. In practice, this means either:
- The vehicle runs with no one in the driver’s seat, or
- The vehicle operates in a mode where no occupant is expected to monitor or intervene.

3. Tesla or any Tesla-affiliated entity must make the unsupervised Full Self-Driving capability broadly accessible either through a robo-taxi or ride-hailing service open to the public, or inclusion as a standard or optional feature on newly sold Tesla vehicles, or a software update that enables unsupervised operation in current vehicles.

Any mode that requires a licensed driver to be seated in the vehicle, or for a human (on-site or remote) to continuously supervise the driving performance or remain ready to intervene at any moment, will not qualify. However, remote monitoring that does not involve continuous supervision or expectation of real-time intervention—for example, oversight limited to emergency response—will not disqualify the service.

This market’s resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.
label: NO
rationale: The evidence indicates Tesla has been operating limited robotaxi services with safety drivers and has discussed removing safety drivers in parts of Austin, but the provided sources do not show a publicly released, broadly accessible unsupervised Full Self-Driving capability meeting the market’s criteria by Oct. 31, 2025. Several reports are forward-looking or promotional, while the more concrete reporting still describes supervised or safety-driver-based operations and notes regulatory limits. Based on the available evidence, a qualifying launch appears more likely than not to have not occurred by the deadline.

question:
Will NVIDIA reach $288 in November?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during November 2025 (ET), any 1-minute candle for NVIDIA (NVDA) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during regular trading hours (ET) will be considered.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the NVIDIA (NVDA) "High" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NVDA/, with the chart settings on "1m" for candle intervals.

Resolution source: https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NVDA/
label: NO
rationale: The evidence shows NVDA trading around the high-100s in October and November 2025, with Yahoo Finance historical data in the 180-196 range on Nov. 12-20. The highest cited analyst target is $250, still well below $288, and there is no evidence in the provided sources of a split or intraday move to $288 or above during November 2025. While NVIDIA has strong momentum and could be volatile around earnings, reaching $288 would require a very large additional rally from the documented levels, so No is more likely.

question:
Will the Government shutdown end by January 31?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes”  if the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) announces that the U.S. federal government is not shut down due to a lapse in appropriations by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

Partial shutdowns count as shutdowns; announcements of office closures due to holidays or inclement weather do not qualify as a shutdown.

The resolution source for this market will be OPM’s Operating Status page (https://www.opm.gov/policy-data-oversight/snow-dismissal-procedures/current-status/).
label: NO
rationale: The evidence suggests a significant risk that the government remains in a shutdown status into late January. OPM materials show that a lapse in appropriations would cause shutdown furloughs, and multiple news/legal sources indicate a funding fight around the October 1, 2025 fiscal-year deadline with little progress and shutdown described as likely/inevitable. However, the evidence does not confirm the actual shutdown status on January 31, so there remains meaningful uncertainty and a non-trivial chance Congress resolves it before then.
